content:
Member-only story

Roadmaps

Database

AI

Artificial Intelligence

Programming

36 min read
Just now

--

Search looks simple from the outside. A database query returns rows. A server handles requests. An API responds in milliseconds.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Behind every one of those interactions is a system someone designed, built, tuned, and operated. That someone understood memory hierarchies, disk I/O, network protocols, consensus algorithms, storage engines, index structures, query planners, and distributed coordination.
This guide is for engineers who want to understand those systems deeply — not just use them.
It covers:

• Computer Architecture

• Operating Systems and Memory Management

• Computer Networking

• Database Internals

• Distributed Systems

• Storage Engines

• PostgreSQL Internals

• Redis Internals

• Kafka Internals

• Search Engineering and Lucene

• Elasticsearch and Information Retrieval

• Vector Databases and Semantic Search

• Retrieval-Augmented Generation

• Kubernetes Internals

• Observability and Performance

• AI Infrastructure
